title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: epitargets R package Economic Policy Institute provides utilities targets pipeline framework. wraps targets tarchetypes add automatic date-tracking alongside pipeline targets age-based cue support API targets.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: change package, re-run:
code: devtools::build_readme() devtools::document()       # Regenerate NAMESPACE and docs from roxygen2 comments devtools::test()            devtools::check() pkgdown::check_pkgdown()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package code lives R/functions.R three main functions: tar_target_date() — wraps tar_target_raw() create primary target plus companion _date target records Sys.Date() primary target runs tar_age_date() — like tar_target_date() adds age-based cue via tarchetypes::tar_cue_age_raw() target re-runs configurable time period (default: 1 day) collect_target_date() — gathers _date targets tibble columns name time create_csv() — thin wrapper around write_csv() returns file path (useful targets format function)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: code uses functions targets (tar_target_raw), tarchetypes (tar_cue_age_raw), readr (write_csv), tibble (tibble).

what: Economic Policy Institute tools for working with targets
title: Economic Policy Institute tools for working with targets
text: epitargets extends {targets} pipeline framework automatic date-tracking age-based cues, convenience functions.

title: Economic Policy Institute tools for working with targets
Economic Policy Institute tools for working with targets

what: Track when targets were last built
title: Economic Policy Institute tools for working with targets
text: tar_target_date() drop-replacement targets::tar_target(). creates target plus companion _date target records ran:
code: targets::tar_dir({   targets::tar_script({     library(epitargets)     list(       tar_target_date(wages, data.frame(year = 2020:2024, wage = c(15, 15, 16, 17, 18)))     )   })   targets::tar_make()   print(targets::tar_read(wages))   print(targets::tar_read(wages_date)) }) #> + wages dispatched #> ✔ wages completed [1ms, 191 B] #> + wages_date dispatched #> ✔ wages_date completed [0ms, 81 B] #> ✔ ended pipeline [117ms, 2 completed, 0 skipped] #>   year wage #> 1 2020   15 #> 2 2021   15 #> 3 2022   16 #> 4 2023   17 #> 5 2024   18 #> [1] "2026-03-13"

title: Economic Policy Institute tools for working with targets
text: tar_age_date() adds age-based cue target automatically re-runs time period. Useful API calls data goes stale:
code: targets::tar_dir({   targets::tar_script({     library(epitargets)     list(       tar_age_date(daily_data, Sys.time(), age = as.difftime(1, units = "days"))     )   })   targets::tar_make()   print(targets::tar_read(daily_data))   print(targets::tar_read(daily_data_date)) }) #> + daily_data dispatched #> ✔ daily_data completed [0ms, 96 B] #> + daily_data_date dispatched #> ✔ daily_data_date completed [0ms, 81 B] #> ✔ ended pipeline [116ms, 2 completed, 0 skipped] #> [1] "2026-03-13 10:35:03 EDT" #> [1] "2026-03-13"

title: Economic Policy Institute tools for working with targets
text: collect_target_date() gathers _date targets single tibble can see glance target last ran:
code: targets::tar_dir({   targets::tar_script({     library(epitargets)     list(       tar_target_date(wages, data.frame(wage = 18)),       tar_age_date(prices, data.frame(price = 3.50)),       targets::tar_target(freshness, collect_target_date(wages_date, prices_date))     )   })   targets::tar_make()   targets::tar_read(freshness) }) #> + prices dispatched #> ✔ prices completed [1ms, 118 B] #> + wages dispatched #> ✔ wages completed [0ms, 118 B] #> + prices_date dispatched #> ✔ prices_date completed [0ms, 81 B] #> + wages_date dispatched #> ✔ wages_date completed [0ms, 81 B] #> + freshness dispatched #> ✔ freshness completed [2ms, 179 B] #> ✔ ended pipeline [142ms, 5 completed, 0 skipped] #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   name   time       #>   <chr>  <date>     #> 1 wages  2026-03-13 #> 2 prices 2026-03-13

title: Collect date targets into a tibble — collect_target_date
text: Gathers values companion _date targets (created tar_target_date() tar_age_date()) tibble::tibble columns name time.

text: ... Date values _date targets. Pass _date target name (e.g., x_date, y_date).

text: tibble::tibble columns name (character) time.

what: Write data to a CSV file and return the file path — create_csv
title: Write data to a CSV file and return the file path — create_csv
text: thin wrapper around readr::write_csv() writes data file returns file path. useful targets format function target value path written file.

title: Write data to a CSV file and return the file path — create_csv

text: data data frame write. file string giving file path write . ... Additional arguments passed readr::write_csv().

title: Write data to a CSV file and return the file path — create_csv
text: file path file, invisibly.

title: Write data to a CSV file and return the file path — create_csv

what: Create an age-cued target with a companion date target — tar_age_date
title: Create an age-cued target with a companion date target — tar_age_date
text: Like tar_target_date() adds age-based cue via tarchetypes::tar_cue_age_raw() target automatically re-runs configurable time period. Useful targets fetch data APIs.

title: Create an age-cued target with a companion date target — tar_age_date
Create an age-cued target with a companion date target — tar_age_date
text: name Symbol, name target. command Expression, R command run target. age difftime object specifying maximum age target re-runs. Defaults 1 day. ... Additional arguments passed targets::tar_target_raw().

title: Create an age-cued target with a companion date target — tar_age_date
text: list two target objects: primary target (age cue) companion _date target.

title: Create an age-cued target with a companion date target — tar_age_date
code: if (identical(Sys.getenv("TAR_EXAMPLES"), "true")) { targets::tar_dir({   targets::tar_script({     library(targets)     list(       epitargets::tar_age_date(x, 1 + 1, age = as.difftime(1, units = "days"))     )   })   targets::tar_make()   targets::tar_read(x)   targets::tar_read(x_date) }) }

what: Read a CSV file as a target — tar_csv_read
title: Read a CSV file as a target — tar_csv_read
text: convenience wrapper around tarchetypes::tar_file_read() CSV files. Creates pair targets: one track file format = "file", another read file readr::read_csv().

title: Read a CSV file as a target — tar_csv_read
Read a CSV file as a target — tar_csv_read
text: name Symbol, name target. command Expression, R code returns file path CSV. .readr_args named list additional arguments passed readr::read_csv(). Defaults list(show_col_types = FALSE). Supplying argument replaces defaults entirely. ... Additional arguments passed targets::tar_target_raw() read target.

title: Read a CSV file as a target — tar_csv_read
text: list two target objects: file-tracking target (name_file) CSV-reading target (name).

title: Read a CSV file as a target — tar_csv_read
code: if (identical(Sys.getenv("TAR_EXAMPLES"), "true")) { targets::tar_dir({   targets::tar_script({     library(targets)     list(       epitargets::tar_csv_read(my_data, "data.csv")     )   })   targets::tar_manifest() }) }

title: Create a target with a companion date target — tar_target_date
code: if (identical(Sys.getenv("TAR_EXAMPLES"), "true")) { targets::tar_dir({   targets::tar_script({     library(targets)     list(       epitargets::tar_target_date(x, 1 + 1)     )   })   targets::tar_make()   targets::tar_read(x)   targets::tar_read(x_date) }) }
